comments: Location B.  Right at the border of how the field is worked.  The the east the hill raises another 5 feet of elevation over a 50-foot span.  Hill falls sharply off to the west and is heavily weeded and unfarmed.  The crop has just been cut.  Stubble is mainly alfalfa with some clover and some annual grass patches nearby.
Location A.  This location is off field and our boundary needs an update.  Field drops sharply from location B which is roughly 10 feet higher.  This location is covered in weeds and grasses.

Location A.  1st year alfalfa. The field was just chopped before my arrival, so I cannot speak to the crop.  The stubble plants are mainly alfalfa, clover, and grass.  Location is on a moderate slope working to a concentrated flow about 50 ft to the north.  The hill to the east rises about 10 feet in elevation over a 100 ft span. To the west the  field is roughly same elevation as current position.  Condition of the stubble looks uniform to the rest of the field.

Location B.  1st year alfalfa.  Crop has just been cut so I am looking at stubble.  Stubble makeup is primarily alfalfa and grass.  This location is close to the beginning of a concentrated flow that drops roughly 5 feet in elevation to the field boundary.  The exit point is 50 ft north of the current location and concentrated flow is roughly 200 ft long.  No visible erosion as field.

Location B.  Field has just been cut and is 1st year alfalfa.  Stubble looks uniform to conditions in the rest of the field.  Hill to the West climbs an additional 8 feet.  The hill peaks out about 30 feet from here.  This location is on the side of a slope.  The slope terminates roughly 20 feet away to the northeast.

Location A.  Crop is in the same condition as location B.  1st year alfalfa that has just had the second cutting come off of it.  The crop is primarily alfalfa with some grass and clover.  This location is near the top of the hill.  To the southwest the hill rises an additional 3 feet in a span of 20 feet.  This location is roughly 5 feet higher in elevation than location B.

Location A.  At a fairly flat point in a slope.  Could be considered a termination point for slop distance. Hill rises about 15 feet to the West.  Hill peaks about 130 feet away.  Field slopes gently to the east from here, working its way to a concentrated flow in the NE corner.   Corn is growing uniform with rest of field, looks good and green with no pest damage and no weed pressure.  There is a higher concentration of trash from cover crop in this immediate area area,  but fairly routine when viewed in the entirety of the field. A couple of skips in the area but nothing excessive.

Location B.  Corn is in similar condition to Location A.  This location is ablut 3 ft higher than Loaction A.  Density of cover crop trash is noticably less than location A.  No weed or insect pressure found.

Location A is in a depression.  The soil appears damper and darker than rest of the field.  The hill to the West peaks roughly 100 feet away and is roughly 10 ft higher than location.  Eastern terrain flatter.  Less than a foot of elevation gain.  Terrain slopes gently to the North.  Corn is green and healthy and spaced in 20" rows.  No insect damage visible and entirely clear of weeds.  Field was in field key as soybeans from triticale but rotation changed to corn. 7 dwarfed plants found in 10x10 square.
Location B.  About 30 feet away from Location A.  Corn is in identical condition to Location A.  Location B is on a slope and roughly 1 foot higher than Location A.
